sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application outlines a clear research vision regarding the care of PLWHIV and identifies a distinctive approach using the Person-Based Approach (PBA) with co-applicants having lived experience. The team composition suggests institutional support through a mix of clinical and research expertise.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's career trajectory or long-term ambitions (g.1.a). No specific training plan is described for the applicant (g.1.c). Mentorship is implied through team roles but lacks a structured description of supervision frequency or specific mentorship activities (g.1.d). The 'distinctive ideas' are strong but the independent direction of the specific applicant is not explicitly detailed beyond the team context.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's career trajectory or long-term ambitions (g.1.a). No specific training plan is described for the applicant (g.1.c). Mentorship is implied through team roles but lacks a structured description of supervision frequency or specific mentorship activities (g.1.d). The 'distinctive ideas' are strong but the independent direction of the specific applicant is not explicitly detailed beyond the team context.

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates existing partnerships with NHS, public health, and charity sectors through the listed collaborators and PPI advisors (g.2.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text focuses entirely on the research project itself and does not contain a personal statement or section explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a, g.2.b). There is no discussion of how the award will build on existing experience or enable new skills for the applicant (g.2.c). The text does not articulate the difference this award makes to the applicant's career trajectory.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text focuses entirely on the research project itself and does not contain a personal statement or section explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a, g.2.b). There is no discussion of how the award will build on existing experience or enable new skills for the applicant (g.2.c). The text does not articulate the difference this award makes to the applicant's career trajectory.

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides strong evidence of relevant prior projects and methods, noting the lead applicant's PhD research involving electronic health records and focus groups, and team members' experience with NIHR grants and scoping reviews (g.3.a, g.3.c). The skills align well with the proposed workstreams.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of formal training courses, certifications, or specific workshops the applicant has completed (g.3.b). The evidence is largely team-based rather than explicitly isolating the lead applicant's individual training history.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of formal training courses, certifications, or specific workshops the applicant has completed (g.3.b). The evidence is largely team-based rather than explicitly isolating the lead applicant's individual training history.

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: The text mentions that key team members have a track record of publications and successful NIHR grant applications (g.4.a, g.4.c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No specific citation metrics, impact factors, or quantitative indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical or societal impact of previous work is generic and not detailed (g.4.d). The evidence is attributed to the team rather than specifically quantifying the lead applicant's individual outputs.
sub_criteria.rationale: No specific citation metrics, impact factors, or quantitative indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical or societal impact of previous work is generic and not detailed (g.4.d). The evidence is attributed to the team rather than specifically quantifying the lead applicant's individual outputs.

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant is listed as a joint lead, suggesting some level of increasing responsibility (g.5.b). The team's recognition by peers is implied through successful grant applications (g.5.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There are no explicit statements of the applicant's career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No participation in leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). The evidence for leadership potential is inferred from the project role rather than explicitly stated as a career trajectory.
sub_criteria.rationale: There are no explicit statements of the applicant's career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No participation in leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). The evidence for leadership potential is inferred from the project role rather than explicitly stated as a career trajectory.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: The application strongly demonstrates involvement in clinical or practice-based research, with GP academics and HIV specialists on the team and a focus on general practice delivery (g.6.b). The team includes members of professional networks like BHIVA (g.6.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of the applicant's intention to pursue a practitioner-academic career (g.6.a). Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities is not detailed for the specific applicant (g.6.c).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of the applicant's intention to pursue a practitioner-academic career (g.6.a). Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities is not detailed for the specific applicant (g.6.c).

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is highly readable (Flesch-Kincaid grade 5.3, reading ease 74.35) with very low jargon density (2.41%). It clearly articulates the problem (ageing PLWHIV, lack of GP guidance), objectives (develop a care model), methods (review, database analysis, co-design), beneficiaries (PLWHIV, GPs), and impact (clinical guidance, healthier lives). The language is simple and accessible, avoiding unexplained technical terms.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the summary is concise, coherent, and perfectly aligned with the detailed research plan.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the summary is concise, coherent, and perfectly aligned with the detailed research plan.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a critical review of existing evidence, citing specific reports (e.g., BHIVA 2017) and identifying limitations such as poor quality evidence for shared-care models and dated research. It clearly identifies knowledge gaps regarding GP guidance and the need for an up-to-date scoping review that includes patient experiences.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the review is comprehensive and logically structured to justify the proposed research.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the review is comprehensive and logically structured to justify the proposed research.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The scope is well-defined with three distinct workstreams (scoping review, database analysis, co-design) and clear timelines (months 1-16). The 18-month duration and £180k budget align well with the complexity of the tasks, including recruitment, data analysis, and stakeholder engagement.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the project scope is appropriate for the award level and timeframe.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the project scope is appropriate for the award level and timeframe.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described: Arksey and O'Malley framework for the scoping review, CPRD Aurum database for quantitative analysis with regression techniques, and Person-Based Approach (PBA) with focus groups for co-design. The alignment between aims and methods is logical and rigorous.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the methodological design is robust and clearly justified.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the methodological design is robust and clearly justified.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly outlines benefits for PLWHIV (longer, healthier lives, better care coordination) and the NHS (effective resource use). It aligns with national priorities (NHS Long Term Plan) and specifies expected outcomes like evidence-based clinical guidance and a pilot-ready care model.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the impact is specific, patient-centered, and system-relevant.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the impact is specific, patient-centered, and system-relevant.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Strong evidence of engagement: PLWHIV are co-applicants, co-lead the steering group, and participate in focus groups. The Person-Based Approach ensures involvement at all stages (design, review, dissemination). Ethical considerations and compensation for participants are explicitly mentioned.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the engagement plan is comprehensive and integrated throughout the research lifecycle.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the engagement plan is comprehensive and integrated throughout the research lifecycle.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The total cost (£180,401.32) and duration (18 months) are clearly stated. The application mentions that compensation for stakeholder time and expenses is costed in the budget, linking resources to specific engagement activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the total cost is provided, there is no detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., staff costs, travel, data access fees) in the provided text to fully justify the proportionality of specific line items. The link between specific tasks and specific resource amounts is implied but not explicitly detailed in the text.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the total cost is provided, there is no detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., staff costs, travel, data access fees) in the provided text to fully justify the proportionality of specific line items. The link between specific tasks and specific resource amounts is implied but not explicitly detailed in the text.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The team demonstrates strong prior experience: successful NIHR grant applications, publications on HIV care, and specific expertise in electronic health records (CPRD), scoping reviews, and PBA. Skills are well-aligned with the proposed work (e.g., RM's PhD on EHRs, SD's PBA workshops).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team's track record is strong, the application does not list specific publication titles or citation counts, which would provide more concrete evidence of output quality (pr.8.a). However, the description of successful grants and specific methodological expertise is sufficient for a high score.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team's track record is strong, the application does not list specific publication titles or citation counts, which would provide more concrete evidence of output quality (pr.8.a). However, the description of successful grants and specific methodological expertise is sufficient for a high score.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly addresses inclusivity by identifying underrepresented groups (MSM, Black African, trans/gender diverse) and planning 'extra efforts' to recruit them. The stakeholder group composition is designed to reflect these demographics, and focus groups include options for online participation to improve accessibility.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks; the inclusive design is explicit and actionable.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks; the inclusive design is explicit and actionable.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: The application mentions that 'Training on PBA will be provided' to the stakeholder group (secdrp__001_i) and notes that team member SD 'runs workshops on PBA' (secdrp__001_g), indicating some awareness of training needs related to the methodology.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit, structured training plan for the applicant or the research team. The mention of training applies to the stakeholder group, not the researchers. There is no timeline, sequencing, or detailed description of training activities for the award holder.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit, structured training plan for the applicant or the research team. The mention of training applies to the stakeholder group, not the researchers. There is no timeline, sequencing, or detailed description of training activities for the award holder.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: The application identifies specific expertise gaps that are addressed by team members (e.g., MTR for statistics, SD for PBA) which implies a form of mentorship or support (secdrp__001_g). It also mentions training for the stakeholder group (secdrp__001_i).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application fails to identify the applicant's personal skill gaps. There is no mapping of training activities to the applicant's development needs. The training mentioned is for stakeholders, not the researcher, and there is no evidence of targeted courses or workshops for the applicant.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application fails to identify the applicant's personal skill gaps. There is no mapping of training activities to the applicant's development needs. The training mentioned is for stakeholders, not the researcher, and there is no evidence of targeted courses or workshops for the applicant.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the application text regarding career aspirations or how the project supports long-term career goals.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application contains no explicit statement of career goals, no connection between the project activities and future career trajectory, and no mention of skill transferability or professional practice components for the applicant.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application contains no explicit statement of career goals, no connection between the project activities and future career trajectory, and no mention of skill transferability or professional practice components for the applicant.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides strong evidence of the team's track record, explicitly mentioning 'successful NIHR grant applications' and 'track record of publications' for key members (JH, WL, JVR). It also highlights specific expertise in relevant fields such as electronic health records, scoping reviews, and HIV care, demonstrating institutional and partner capability.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While individual team member achievements are well-documented, the application does not explicitly detail the broader contracting organisation's (Integrated Care Board) specific institutional research history or awards separate from the individual team members' credentials.
sub_criteria.rationale: While individual team member achievements are well-documented, the application does not explicitly detail the broader contracting organisation's (Integrated Care Board) specific institutional research history or awards separate from the individual team members' credentials.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly defines specific roles and responsibilities for supervisors and advisors (e.g., MTR for statistics, SD for PBA, ANe for PPI advice). There is strong alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs. A 'Study Steering Group' is established for day-to-day management, and a stakeholder group meets every four months, providing a structured framework for oversight.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structure of the steering group is defined, the specific frequency and format of direct one-on-one supervision or mentorship meetings for the applicant (Dr. Richard Ma) are not explicitly detailed beyond the group meetings.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the structure of the steering group is defined, the specific frequency and format of direct one-on-one supervision or mentorship meetings for the applicant (Dr. Richard Ma) are not explicitly detailed beyond the group meetings.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The application identifies the contracting organisation as an 'INTEGRATED CARE BOARD' and notes that GP academics on the team will provide 'access to networks of primary care clinicians for study recruitment,' linking organisational support to project success. The team composition suggests a supportive environment for practitioner-academic research.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is very limited specific evidence regarding the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the Integrated Care Board itself. The application does not explicitly describe how the organisation supports the specific award type (e.g., doctoral vs. postdoctoral) or details tailored career development support mechanisms beyond the project team.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is very limited specific evidence regarding the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the Integrated Care Board itself. The application does not explicitly describe how the organisation supports the specific award type (e.g., doctoral vs. postdoctoral) or details tailored career development support mechanisms beyond the project team.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a commitment to inclusivity by explicitly planning to ensure the stakeholder group reflects demographics most affected by HIV (MSM, Black African, trans) and those who might be underrepresented. It mentions 'extra efforts' to recruit underserved groups and addresses barriers like stigma and racism.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (recruitment strategies) rather than describing broader organisational policies, training initiatives, or a formal culture of research integrity and support for underrepresented groups within the contracting organisation itself.
sub_criteria.rationale: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (recruitment strategies) rather than describing broader organisational policies, training initiatives, or a formal culture of research integrity and support for underrepresented groups within the contracting organisation itself.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly integrates lived experience via the Person-Based Approach (PBA), with PLWHIV co-applicants co-leading the stakeholder group and steering group. Specific activities include co-designing the care model, reviewing results, and providing feedback on study materials. The plan details how input will shape the intervention design.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for co-design is robust, specific examples of *changes already made* to the study design based on prior lived experience are not detailed (as this is a proposal for future work), though the mechanism for making such changes is clearly defined.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for co-design is robust, specific examples of *changes already made* to the study design based on prior lived experience are not detailed (as this is a proposal for future work), though the mechanism for making such changes is clearly defined.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The research team includes two co-applicants with lived experience of HIV (ANa, THI) who are also in the charity sector, ensuring diverse perspectives. The team composition explicitly includes PLWHIV, GPs, HIV specialists, and researchers, indicating equitable collaboration across different roles.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of the team's demographic diversity (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age) beyond the inclusion of PLWHIV. Statements about inclusion in hiring are absent, as the team appears pre-formed rather than being recruited for this specific award.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of the team's demographic diversity (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age) beyond the inclusion of PLWHIV. Statements about inclusion in hiring are absent, as the team appears pre-formed rather than being recruited for this specific award.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: A comprehensive dissemination strategy is outlined, targeting professionals, policymakers, and the public via journals, conferences, social media, and press releases. The link between objectives and benefits is clear: generating evidence for clinical guidance to improve care for PLWHIV. The plan emphasizes actionable outcomes like a model of care ready for piloting.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the dissemination channels are diverse, specific metrics for measuring the success of public engagement or the uptake of findings by the public are not detailed.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the dissemination channels are diverse, specific metrics for measuring the success of public engagement or the uptake of findings by the public are not detailed.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies seldom-heard groups (MSM, Black African, trans/gender diverse) and acknowledges their disproportionate impact. It details targeted recruitment strategies, including 'extra efforts' to find representation from underserved demographics in focus groups and the stakeholder group. Barriers like stigma and racism are discussed as factors to be addressed.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While barriers are identified and strategies mentioned, specific mitigation tactics for overcoming these barriers during recruitment (beyond 'extra efforts') could be more detailed.
sub_criteria.rationale: While barriers are identified and strategies mentioned, specific mitigation tactics for overcoming these barriers during recruitment (beyond 'extra efforts') could be more detailed.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the lifecycle: planning (PBA, stakeholder group), conduct (focus groups with diverse participants), and dissemination (PPI involvement in sharing findings). Accessibility is considered through online options for meetings and reimbursement for expenses.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Explicit plans for ongoing evaluation of the inclusivity of the research process itself (e.g., feedback loops on the PPI experience) are less detailed than the plans for the research outputs.
sub_criteria.rationale: Explicit plans for ongoing evaluation of the inclusivity of the research process itself (e.g., feedback loops on the PPI experience) are less detailed than the plans for the research outputs.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly states that compensation for time and expenses for stakeholder group members and focus group participants is costed in the budget. Staff time for PPI advisors and co-applicants is clearly allocated to lead engagement activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the text confirms costs are included, it does not provide a specific budget breakdown or line-item amounts for these inclusion activities, relying on the statement that they are 'costed'.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the text confirms costs are included, it does not provide a specific budget breakdown or line-item amounts for these inclusion activities, relying on the statement that they are 'costed'.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates excellent structural organization with 10 numbered heading-like lines and 22 bullet/numbered list markers, creating a clear hierarchy. The parser detected 13 emphasis markers, indicating effective use of bolding or italics for key terms. The section coverage is distinct and well-defined across the Plain English Summary, Detailed Research Plan, and Application Context.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While emphasis markers are present, the parser notes that bold/emphasis may be lost during text extraction, meaning the visual impact of these markers cannot be fully verified in the raw text provided, though the structural analysis confirms their presence.
sub_criteria.rationale: While emphasis markers are present, the parser notes that bold/emphasis may be lost during text extraction, meaning the visual impact of these markers cannot be fully verified in the raw text provided, though the structural analysis confirms their presence.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: The structural analysis explicitly reports a duplicate sentence rate of 0.0 and a repeated line rate of 0.0. No high cross-section lexical overlap was detected, indicating that the content is novel and free from unnecessary repetition across the different sections of the application.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application exhibits strong logical flow with 42 objective-method-budget link terms and 7 transition phrases. The section order follows a standard, logical progression from context to summary to detailed plan. The narrative thread is anchored clearly on the research aims, with explicit links between the problem statement, objectives, and the three workstreams.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structural analysis confirms the presence of linking terms and transitions, the semantic coherence (af.3.a) is inferred from these counts rather than a direct embedding analysis of the full text, though the evidence is strong.
